sections.title: A quest for the rune platebody
Talking to the Guildmaster again before talking to Oziach

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Greetings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a quest?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: You're already on a quest for me, if I recall correctly. Have you talked to Oziach yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Well, he's the only one who can grant you the right to wear rune platemail. He lives in a hut, by the cliffs west of Edgeville.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll go and talk to him.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Greetings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: This is the Champions' Guild. Only adventurers who have proved themselves worthy by gaining influence from quests are allowed in here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a quest?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Aha!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Yes! A mighty and perilous quest fit only for the most powerful champions! And, at the end of it, you will earn the right to wear the legendary rune platebody!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what is this quest?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: You'll have to speak to Oziach, the maker of rune armour. He sets the quests that champions must complete in order to wear it.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Oziach lives in a hut, by the cliffs to the west of Edgeville. He can be a little...odd...sometimes, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'll give it a miss.

sections.title: Talking to Oziach before slaying the dragon

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Have ye slayed that dragon yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... no.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Be off with ye then.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Aye, 'tis a fair day my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you sell me a rune platebody?

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: So, how does thee know I 'ave some?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Guildmaster of the Champions' Guild told me.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Yes, I suppose he would, wouldn't he? He's always sending you fancy-pants 'heroes' up to bother me. Telling me I'll give them a quest or sommat like that.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Well, I'm not going to let just anyone wear my rune platemail. It's only for heroes. So, leave me alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you were going to give me a quest.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: *Sigh*

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: All right, I'll give ye a quest. I'll let ye wear my rune platemail if ye...

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Slay the dragon of Crandor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A dragon, that sounds like fun!

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Hah, yes, you are a typical reckless adventurer, aren't you? Now go kill the dragon and get out of my face.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how can I defeat the dragon?

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Go talk to the Guildmaster in the Champions' Guild. He'll help ye out if yer so keen on doing a quest. I'm not going to be handholding any adventurers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may be a champion, but I don't think I'm up to dragon-killing yet.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Yes, I can understand that. Yer a coward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a pity, I'm not a hero.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Aye, I ken tell!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a master detective.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Well, however you found out about it, I'm not going to let just anyone wear my rune platemail. It's only for heroes. So leave me alone.

sections.title: Talking to the Guildmaster after talking to Oziach

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Greetings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I talked to Oziach and he gave me a quest.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Oh? What did he tell you to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Defeat the dragon of Crandor.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: The dragon of Crandor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um, yes...

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Goodness, he hasn't given you an easy job, has he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's so special about this dragon?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Thirty years ago, Crandor was a thriving community with a great tradition of mages and adventurers. Many Crandorians even earned the right to be part of the Champions' Guild!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: One of their adventurers went too far, however. He descended into the volcano in the centre of Crandor and woke the dragon Elvarg.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: He must have fought valiantly against the dragon because they say that, to this day, she has a scar down her side,

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: but the dragon still won the fight. She emerged and laid waste to the whole of Crandor with her fire breath!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Some refugees managed to escape in fishing boats. They landed on the coast, north of Rimmington, and set up camp but the dragon followed them and burned the camp to the ground.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Out of all the people of Crandor there were only three survivors: a trio of wizards who used magic to escape. Their names were Thalzar, Lozar and Melzar.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: If you're serious about taking on Elvarg, first you'll need to get to Crandor, and to my knowledge, the only way to do that is by sea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just sail over there? Seems easy enough.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: If only that were true. The island is surrounded by dangerous underwater reefs. You'll need a map to guide you through them and a ship capable of doing so.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: And then of course there's the dragon herself. Once you reach Crandor, you'll need some kind of protection against her breath.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find a map to Crandor?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: After what happened, people were afraid of enraging the dragon. Most maps to the island were destroyed, but I have heard that one survived. It was held by Melzar, Thalzar and Lozar.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: However, the three apparently split the map into three pieces, which each of them taking one piece.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Melzar's map piece?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Melzar built a castle on the site of the Crandorian refugee camp, north of Rimmington. He's locked himself in there and no one's seen him for years.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: The inside of his castle is like a maze, and is populated by undead monsters. Maybe, if you could get all the way through the maze, you could find his piece of the map.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Adventurers sometimes go in there to prove themselves, so I can give you this key to Melzar's Maze.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Thalzar's map piece?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Thalzar was the most paranoid of the three wizards. He hid his map piece and took the secret of its location to his grave.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: I don't think you'd be able to find out where it is by ordinary means. You'll need to talk to the Oracle on Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Lozar's map piece?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: A few weeks ago, I'd have told you to speak to Lozar herself, in her house across the river from Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Unfortunately, goblin raiders killed her and stole everything. One of the goblins from the Goblin Village probably has the map piece now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of ship do I need?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Quite a specific one I'm afraid. Even with a map, very few ships will be able to navigate the reefs around the island. You're going to need one of Crandorian design.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: If there's still one in existence, it's probably in Port Sarim. However, a ship will also need a captain, and I'm not sure where you'd find one willing to sail to Crandor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I not just captain it myself? I have some experience in that area.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: I suppose you could, but given you have a dragon to be dealing with, I'd suggest getting someone else to worry about the captaining for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I protect myself from the dragon's breath?

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: That part shouldn't be too difficult, actually. I believe the Duke of Lumbridge has a special shield in his armoury that is enchanted against dragon's breath.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get going!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Greetings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About my quest to kill the dragon...

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: If you're serious about taking on Elvarg, first you'll need to get to Crandor, and to my knowledge, the only way to do that is by sea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just sail over there? Seems easy enough.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: If only that were true. The island is surrounded by dangerous underwater reefs. You'll need a map to guide you through them and a ship capable of doing so.

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: And then of course there's the dragon herself. Once you reach Crandor, you'll need some kind of protection against her breath.

sections.title: Using any key on the correct door

lines.speaker: Melzar the Mad
lines.text: By the power of custard!

lines.speaker: Melzar the Mad
lines.text: Feel the wrath of my feet!

lines.speaker: Melzar the Mad
lines.text: Let me drink my tea in peace.

lines.speaker: Melzar the Mad
lines.text: Leave me alone, I need to feed my pet rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek a piece of the map to the island of Crandor.

lines.speaker: Oracle
lines.text: The map's behind a door below, but entering is rather tough. This is what you need to know: You must use the following stuff.

lines.speaker: Oracle
lines.text: First, a drink used by a mage. Next, some worm string, changed to sheet. Then, a small crustacean cage. Last, a bowl that's not seen heat.

sections.title: Using respective items on the door

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard that one of your number has got hold of part of a map.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Aha, that'd be Wormbrain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would he be?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Wormbrain steals too much. He got caught. Now he lives in Port Sarim town jail.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Whut you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe you've got piece of a map that I need.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: So? Why should I be giving it to you? What you do for Wormbrain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not going to do anything for you. Forget it.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Be dat way then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll let you live. I could just kill you.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Ha! Me in here and you out dere. You not get map piece.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I could pay you for the map piece. Say, 500 coins?

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Me not stoopid, it worth at least 10,000 coins!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must be joking! Forget it.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Fine, you not get map piece.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright then, 10,000 coins it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops, I don't have enough on me.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Comes back when you has enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright then, 10,000 coins it is.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Fank you very much! Now me can bribe da guards, hehehe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you get the map piece from?

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: We rob house of stupid wizard. She very old, not put up much fight at all. Hahaha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... Hahaha.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Her house full of pictures of a city on island and old pictures of people. Me not recognise island.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Me find map piece. Me not know what it is, but it in locked box so me figure it important.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: But, by the time me get box open, other goblins gone. Then me not run fast enough and guards catch me.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: But now you want map piece so must be special! What you do for me to get it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you in for?

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Me not sure. Me pick some stuff up and take it away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, did the stuff belong to you?

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Umm...no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that would be why then.

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Oh, right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, thought this was a zoo.

sections.title: Trying to attack him while having his map piece
Trying to attack him without his map piece after having just defeated him

lines.speaker: Wormbrain
lines.text: Ow!

sections.title: Using a map piece on another while only having 2
Attempting to board the ship before purchasing it

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Hey, stay off my ship! That's private property!

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Hello there! Interested in buying this fine ship? I'm sure I can offer her to you for a much better price than any of those shipwrights ask for!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A much better price? What's the catch?

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Well, she's not quite seaworthy right now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not seaworthy? Why would I want to buy her then?

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Because this isn't just any ship! This is the Lady Lumbridge, one of the last Crandorian ships! Despite her size, she's one of the most agile ship around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Except she's not seaworthy...

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: No, but a bit of work will quickly sort that out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to buy her.

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Of course! I'm sure the work needed to do on it wouldn't be too expensive.

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: How does 2,000 gold sound? I'll even throw in my cabin boy, Jenkins, for free! He'll swab the decks and splice the mainsails for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep, sounds good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...except I don't have that kind of money on me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep, sounds good.

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Okey-dokey! She's all yours!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not paying that much for a broken boat!

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: That's fair enough, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is she damaged?

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Oh, there was no particular accident, just years of wear and tear. It's been a long time since Crandor was destroyed and she's had several owners since then. Unfortunately, not all of them looked after her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll pass.

sections.title: Talking to Klarense after purchasing the Lady Lumbridge

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Hello, captain! Here to inspect your new ship? Just a little work and she'll be seaworthy again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what needs fixing on this ship?

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Well, the big gaping hole in the hold is the problem. You'll need a few wooden planks hammered in with steel nails to fix it.

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: I dunno. It looks fine to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you said it needed fix-

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Yes, but that was before you fixed it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So would you like to sail this ship to Crandor for me?

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Crandor? You're joking, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Crandor's not something we sailors joke about. You can't sail from here to Catherby, or Entrana, or Ardougne without going past that accursed island.

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: You can't get close to it because of the reefs, but you can always see it.

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Sometimes you can see a dark shape in the sky, circling the island. That's when you have to sail on as quick as you can and pray it's not hungry. Every year, more ships are lost to that dragon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I want to go to Crandor.

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Then you must be crazy.

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: That island is surrounded by reefs that would rip this ship to shreds. Even if you found a map, you'd need an experienced captain to stand a chance of getting through

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: and, even if I could get to it, there's no way I'm going any closer to that dragon than I have to. They say it can destroy whole ships with one bite.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you going to do now you don't have a ship?

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Oh, I'll be fine. I've got work as Port Sarim's first lifeguard!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I board the ship now?

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Sure thing, she's all yours.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Ahoy! What d'ye think of yer ship then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready to go back on shore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to inspect her some more.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Aye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you sail this ship to Crandor?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Not me, sir! I'm just an 'umble cabin boy. You'll need a proper cap'n.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find a captain?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: The cap'ns round 'ere seem to be a mite scared of Crandor. I ask 'em why and they just say it was afore my time,

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: but there is one cap'n I reckon might 'elp. I 'eard there's a retired 'un who lives in Draynor Village who's so desperate to sail again 'e'd take any job.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: I can't remember 'is name, but 'e lives in Draynor Village an' makes rope.

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Arrr, matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me get to Crandor Island?

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Arrr, matey, ye canna be serious! That be the home of Elvarg the Dragon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's right. I 've got to go there and kill the dragon for this quest I'm doing.

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: I canna help ye. And right sorry I be to see a fine young [laddie/lassie/'un] like yerself go off to yer doom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, but I'll be fine!

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Arrr...

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Arrr, matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me get to Crandor Island?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Crandor Island, ye say?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Arrr, keep yeself away from that accursed place if ye values yer skin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that's a 'No', then?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Aye, that it be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anyone who would take me there?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: No, I don't know many idiots. Except for young Ben there.

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: Arrr, get away with ye, ye scurvy scallywag!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: Arrr, matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me get to Crandor Island?

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: Arrr, ye may be wanting to go to Crandor Island now, but if ye gets there, ye'll be a-wantin' to get off it again quickly enough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that's a 'No', then?

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: [Aye, laddie/Aye, lassie/Aye], that it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anyone who would take me there?

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: There's only one sailor alive daft enough to go there. Old Ned, the ropemaker in Draynor Village. I can't think of any other fools just now. Except for old Jack Seagull there.

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Arrr, who are ye calling old?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Seaman Lorris
lines.text: Hello there. Do you want to go on a trip to Karamja? We can take you to Musa Point for only 30 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather go to Crandor.

lines.speaker: Seaman Lorris/Thresnor/Captain Tobias
lines.text: Crandor? No chance! You may have a death wish, but we don't!

lines.speaker: Trader Stan
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I would like to charter a ship.

lines.speaker: Trader Stan
lines.text: [Certainly sir/Certainly madam/Certainly]. Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Trader Stan
lines.text: Crandor? Are you crazy? No charter ship would take you to that island. You'd need to use your own ship, but even then, you'd need a miracle to find a safe route there!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Why, hello there, [lad/lass/kid]. Me friends call me Ned. I was a man of the sea, but it's past me now. Could I be making or selling you some rope?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a sailor? Could you take me to the island of Crandor?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Well, I was a sailor. I've not been able to get work at sea these days, though. They say I'm too old.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Sorry, where was it you said you wanted to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To the island of Crandor.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Crandor?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: But... It would be a chance to sail a ship once more. I'd sail anywhere if it was a chance to sail again.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Then again, no captain in his right mind would sail to that island...

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Ah, you only live once! I'll do it!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: So, where's your ship?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't actually have a ship yet.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Ah, well. Let me know when you do. The place to look would be Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's the Lady Lumbridge, in Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: That old pile of junk? Last I heard, she wasn't seaworthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I fixed her up!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: You did? Excellent!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Just show me the map and we can get ready to go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Excellent! I'll meet you at the ship, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Excellent! I'll meet you at the ship, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...yeah...about that. I don't actually have a map on me.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: You'd better go find it, then. I'm not going to go sailing off without a map to show me where to head!

sections.title: Talking to Ned again if the player wasn't ready the first time

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Hello again, [lad/lass/kid]. Something I can help you with? Want to buy some rope perhaps?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you take me to Crandor now, then?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: I said I would and old Ned is a man of his word!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: So, where's your ship?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't actually have a ship yet.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Ah, well. Let me know when you do. The place to look would be Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's the Lady Lumbridge, in Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: That old pile of junk? Last I heard, she wasn't seaworthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, good point. I'd better go and fix her up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I fixed her up!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: You did? Excellent!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Just show me the map and we can get ready to go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Excellent! I'll meet you at the ship, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...yeah...about that. I don't actually have a map on me.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: You'd better go find it, then. I'm not going to go sailing off without a map to show me where to head!

sections.title: Talking to Cabin Boy Jenkins on the ship

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Ahoy! What d'ye think of yer ship then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready to go back on shore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to inspect her some more.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Aye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you sail this ship to Crandor?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Not me, sir! I'm just an 'umble cabin boy. You'll need a proper cap'n.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find a captain?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: The cap'ns round 'ere seem to be a mite scared of Crandor. I ask 'em why and they just say it was afore my time,

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: but there is one cap'n I reckon might 'elp. I 'eard there's a retired 'un who lives in Draynor Village who's so desperate to sail again 'e'd take any job.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: I can't remember 'is name, but 'e lives in Draynor Village an' makes rope.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek a shield that will protect me from dragonbreath.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: A knight going on a dragon quest, hmm? What dragon do you intend to slay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elvarg, the dragon of Crandor island!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Elvarg? Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Well, you're a braver man than I!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is everyone so scared of this dragon?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Back in my father's day, Crandor was an important city-state. Politically, it was as important as Falador or Varrock and its ships traded with every port.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: But, one day when I was little, all contact was lost. The trading ships and the diplomatic envoys just stopped coming.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I remember my father being very scared. He posted lookouts on the roof to warn if the dragon was approaching. All the city rulers were worried that Elvarg would devastate the whole continent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better leave that dragon alone.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: That's a relief. I would hate to see such a promising adventurer cut down in [his/her/their] prime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, are you going to give me the shield or not?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: If you really think you're up to it then perhaps you are the one who can kill this dragon.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Take care out there. If you kill it...

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: If you kill it, for Saradomin's sake make sure it's really dead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Very wise. There are some monsters that are best left alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, no dragon in particular. I just feel like killing a dragon.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I don't have an infinite supply of these shields, you know. I'll only give one for a truly worthy cause.

sections.title: Talking to Ned after giving him the Crandor map

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Hello again, [lad/lass/kid]. Something I can help you with? Want to buy some rope perhaps?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you take me to Crandor now, then?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: I said I would and old Ned is a man of his word! I'll meet you on board the Lady Lumbridge in Port Sarim.

sections.title: Talking to Cabin Boy Jenkins while Ned is on board

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Arr, Jim lad!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: It's Jenkins, cap'n!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Arr, Jenkins lad!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Shiver me timbers!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Aye aye, cap'n!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Splice the mainsail!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Aye aye, cap'n!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Swab the deck!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Aye aye, cap'n!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Weigh the anchor!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: It's too heavy, cap'n!

sections.title: Talking to Ned on the Lady Lumbridge

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Ah, it's good to be on board a ship again! No matter how long I live on land, a ship will always seem better. Are you ready to depart?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not quite ready yet.

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Well, you go do whatever you need to do. I'll wait here for you until you're ready.

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Ah, it's good to feel the salt spray on my face once again!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: And this is a mighty fine ship. She don't look much, but she handles like a dream.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much longer until we reach Crandor?

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Not long now! According to the chart, we'd be able to see Crandor if it wasn't for those clouds on the horizon.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Jenkins
lines.text: Looks like there's a storm coming up, cap'n. Soon we won't be able to see anything!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Oh, well. The weather had been so good up until now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see that?

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: See what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought I saw something up above us.

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: It's the dragon!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: We're going to sink!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look! Land ahead!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: We're going to crash!

sections.title: Talking to Ned after reaching Crandor

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Now I see why all the other captains said I'd be mad to go near Crandor. It looks like we're stranded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry about that. I found a secret passage in the dragon's lair that leads off the island!

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: Oh, that's good. I'll be able to get back to Draynor somehow,

lines.speaker: Captain Ned
lines.text: if I can sneak past all the skeletons and demons on this island!

sections.title: Talking to Klarense after reaching Crandor

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: Wow! You sure are lucky! Seems the Lady Lumbridge just washed right up into the dock by herself!

lines.speaker: Klarense
lines.text: She's pretty badly damaged, though...

sections.title: Talking to the Guildmaster after slaying Elvarg

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Greetings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I killed the dragon!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: Really? That's amazing!

lines.speaker: Guildmaster
lines.text: You are a true hero. There's no way that Oziach can refuse to sell you rune platemail now. You should tell him at once!

sections.title: Talking to Oziach after slaying Elvarg

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Have ye slayed that dragon yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have its head here.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: You actually did it?

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: I underestimated ye, adventurer. I apologise.

lines.speaker: Oziach
lines.text: Yer a true hero, and I'd be happy to sell ye rune platebodies.
